content: When you send a secret message to your friend, you want to make sure only they can read it, right? That's where encryption comes in. Encryption is like a special code that scrambles your message so only the right person can unscramble it. Imagine you're sending a letter to your friend, but instead of writing the real words, you replace each letter with a different one. Like, all the "a"s become "d"s and all the "b"s become "e"s. It would be hard for someone else to figure out what you're saying! That's kind of like what encryption does with messages on the internet. It changes the words and letters into a secret code that only the person with the right "key" can decode. This way, even if someone else tries to read your message, they won't be able to understand it. Isn't that cool?
